name: ForgeOS Trust Index
tagline: We don't have a #1. We have your #1.
description_short: Context-aware trust scoring for open-source packages. Scores tailored to your security posture, team size, and tech stack.
description_long:
The ForgeOS Trust Index (FTI) evaluates open-source packages across
8 scoring dimensions — security, maintainability, documentation,
community health, supply chain, improvement velocity, governance,
and operational. Unlike static rankings,
FTI provides context-aware recommendations based on YOUR specific
needs: security posture, team size, preferred language, and runtime.

FTI is the first trust infrastructure that answers "is this package
right for ME?" instead of "is this package popular?"

tiers.name: Agent Pro
tiers.tagline: Built for AI agents and automation

dimensions.name: Security
dimensions.description: Known vulnerabilities, CVE count, vulnerability patch cadence, SAST findings, signed releases

dimensions.name: Maintainability
dimensions.description: Commit frequency, test coverage, code complexity, open issue age, changelog/CI health

dimensions.name: Documentation
dimensions.description: API reference completeness, README quality, examples, changelog, docs site freshness

dimensions.name: Community Health
dimensions.description: Contributor count, bus factor resilience, issue response time, star/fork trajectory, downstream dependents

dimensions.name: Supply Chain
dimensions.description: Dependency count, transitive CVEs, SBOM presence, Sigstore/in-toto attestations, typosquatting distance

dimensions.name: Improvement Velocity
dimensions.description: 90-day trailing FTI score delta, CVE closure rate, test coverage changes, documentation improvements

dimensions.name: Governance
dimensions.description: CI/system health grade, gate completion rate, separation of duties, ledger integrity, violation remediation

dimensions.name: Operational
dimensions.description: Uptime SLA, incident frequency, MTTR, monitoring coverage, API p95 latency

faq.q: How is FTI different from npm audit or Snyk?
faq.a: Those tools check for known vulnerabilities. FTI evaluates the overall trustworthiness of a package across 8 dimensions — security is just one. FTI also provides context-aware scores tailored to your specific needs.
Yes. Package maintainers can submit disputes through our dispute process. We review all disputes within 5 business days.
Scores are recalculated daily for active packages and on-demand via API. Historical scores are retained for trend analysis.
The public scoreboard and badge embeds are free. API access starts at $99/year for developers, or $29/month for AI agents.
